name: Serra Perenxisa Circuit
name_original: La Serra Perenxisa — Paseo circular desde Torrent
description: Cycle a moderate 23.8-mile loop through Serra Perenxisa Natural Park, enjoying panoramic views near Torrent, Valencia.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Torrent offer a diverse landscape, featuring a mix of paved and unpaved sections suitable for various fitness levels. The region is characterized by natural parks like Serra Perenxisa, providing scenic views and varied terrains. Cyclists can encounter areas with significant elevation gains as well as flatter paths. This makes Torrent an appealing destination for outdoor activities. Best touring cycling routes around Torrent The most popular touring cycling route is Serra Perenxisa Circuit, a 23.8 miles (38.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Serra Perenxisa Natural Park, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Channel on the Magre River – Balcón de Montroy Pass loop from Torrent Avinguda, a difficult 63.9 miles (102.8 km) path. This route primarily features paved surfaces and requires good fitness due to its substantial climbing. Local touring cyclists also love the Balcón de Montroy Pass – Torrent bike path loop from Torrent, a 51.5 miles (82.8 km) trail leading through the Balcón de Montroy Pass, often completed in about 5 hours 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Torrent is defined by natural parks, diverse terrains with both paved and unpaved sections, and notable passes like Balcón de Montroy. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging excursions. The routes in Torrent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Torrent?
A: Torrent offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 35 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Torrent?
A: The terrain around Torrent is diverse, featuring a mix of paved and unpaved sections. You can expect routes with significant elevation gains, particularly in areas like the Balcón de Montroy Pass, as well as flatter paths. Natural parks such as Serra Perenxisa contribute to varied and scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Torrent?
A: Yes, Torrent has several easy touring cycling routes. There are 9 routes specifically categorized as easy, perfect for those new to touring cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride. An example of a moderate route that is accessible is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40372766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Alter Roman Villa loop from Torrent Avinguda&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 26.4 km.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Torrent?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Torrent offers 14 difficult touring cycling routes. These often feature substantial climbing and longer distances. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Channel on the Magre River – Balcón de Montroy Pass loop from Torrent Avinguda&lt;/a&gt;, a 102.8 km route with significant elevation.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along touring cycling routes near Torrent?
A: Many touring cycling routes in the Torrent area pass by or are close to notable natural attractions. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4058748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de Galleç Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4254465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de l'Horteta&lt;/a&gt; gorge, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6716796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de l'Algoder&lt;/a&gt; natural monument. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918695558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Perenxisa Circuit&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views within a natural park setting.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Torrent?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Torrent are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918695558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Perenxisa Circuit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1671771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Montroy Pass – Torrent bike path loop from Torrent&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular circular options.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Torrent?
A: The touring cycling routes in Torrent are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of paved and unpaved sections, and the opportunities to explore natural parks and scenic passes.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent opportunities for scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918695558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Perenxisa Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is known for its panoramic vistas. Additionally, highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2593714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Estepar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; can be incorporated into or found near various routes, offering stunning perspectives of the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Torrent?
A: Torrent's climate generally allows for touring cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer. However, the diverse landscape means you can find suitable routes in different seasons, depending on your preference for warmth or cooler weather.

Q: Are there any coastal touring cycling routes near Torrent?
A: While Torrent itself is inland, the region offers access to coastal cycling experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32104334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal bike path – Torrent bike path loop from Torrent&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.8 km route that connects to coastal areas, providing a different type of scenery. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/977301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bicycle lane along the dunes&lt;/a&gt; further afield, offering beautiful seaside rides.
tips: Nice and colorful waterfall over the ravine. It is located on the path and it allows you to walk over it. It is a very quiet and picturesque place.

The antennas of the Cumbre de Calicanto in the Serra Perenxisa offer wonderful views of the city of Valencia, the Levante coast and the Hoya de Buñol. There are several alternatives to get there, either by paved road or a gravel track with greater difficulty. Either way you will have a nice reward when you get up there!

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this circular route?
faqs.answer: While the route is generally described as having mostly paved surfaces, some segments may be unpaved and challenging. For comfort and navigability, a hybrid or mountain touring bike is generally more suitable than a traditional road touring bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, reflecting its location within the Serra Perenxisa mountain range. You'll encounter mostly paved surfaces, but be prepared for some unpaved sections that might require extra effort or even dismounting your bike. The route includes elevation changes, contributing to its moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers natural landscapes within the Serra Perenxisa Natural Municipal Park. Key highlights include the Barranc de Galleç Waterfall and the Calicanto Antennas – View of Valencia. From higher points, you can enjoy panoramic views of the Valencian coastal plain, including sights of L'Albufera, the port of Valencia, and even the City of Arts and Sciences.

faqs.question: Is this circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within natural parks like Serra Perenxisa, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Please ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring or autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in this region, making cycling less comfortable, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees for cycling in Serra Perenxisa Natural Municipal Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access or cycle within the Paratge Natural Municipal Serra Perenxisa. It is a publicly accessible natural park.

faqs.question: Where does this circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Torrent. While specific parking areas are not detailed, Torrent is a municipality with various parking options. Many users start from urbanizations like Santo Domingo or Calicanto Monte Levant, where street parking might be available. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers parts of the E-2 Torrent - Barri Sant Gregori - Càmping La Piràmide, the PR-CV 162 - Camí de la serra, and the E-1 Urbanització El Pantà - Urbanització Calicanto, among others.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: Komoot categorizes this as a moderate bike ride. This means it requires good fitness and some experience with varied terrain. While much of it is paved, be prepared for some challenging unpaved sections and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty and potential for unpaved, challenging sections, this specific route might be more suitable for families with older, experienced cycling children rather than very young ones or beginners. There may be easier, dedicated family-friendly routes within the broader Serra Perenxisa area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring cycling route, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation. Given the varied terrain, a helmet is essential.
